pattern: The smallest achievable maximum-subarray-sum is monotonic, so binary searching the candidate cap and greedily counting the segments it forces turns optimization into a feasibility test.
hints: If you guess a ceiling for the largest allowed segment sum, you can greedily walk the array and count how many segments that ceiling requires.
A higher ceiling never needs more segments, so the feasibility of 'split into at most k parts' is monotonic in the ceiling.
Binary search the ceiling between the largest single element and the total sum; the smallest ceiling that needs at most k segments is the answer.

edge_inputs.description: single segment forces the full sum
as many parts as elements isolates the maximum
a single large element dominates the answer
zeros mixed in do not change the cap

stress.description: random nonnegative array with k in a valid range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(800, size))
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, min(50, n))
    return [nums, k]
stress.note: k bounded by n so a valid split always exists
